evals.prompt: Verify that the sidebar UI renders correctly after my changes
evals.expected_output: Skill checks for playwright-cli, detects dev server command from package.json, starts the server, spawns browser-verifier agent, captures screenshot, writes verification-result.json, and presents pass/fail summary
expectations: Dev server command is detected from package.json scripts (dev, start, or serve)
Output includes verification-result.json, screenshot.png, and verification-summary.md

evals.prompt: Run browser verification on my React app at http://localhost:3000
evals.expected_output: Skill uses the provided URL directly instead of detecting from package.json, spawns browser-verifier agent, and always cleans up sessions and processes on completion
expectations: Provided URL http://localhost:3000 is used directly
Browser-verifier agent is spawned with correct session naming
Cleanup runs even if verification fails — session closed and dev server killed
Verification failure is treated as a signal, not a hard blocker

evals.expected_output: If playwright-cli is not installed, skill warns gracefully and skips without erroring. If installed, full verification flow executes with headless mode by default.
expectations: Graceful skip when playwright-cli is not installed — warning message, no error
Headless mode is used by default (no --headed flag)
Artifacts are stored in .prism/local/ (gitignored, never committed)
Session naming follows verify-{story-id} or verify-{timestamp} convention
